objective: In this paper, we look at the challenges of snapshot acquisition and analysis .
In this paper, we intend to solve the challenges of investigation when the VM snapshots are used as the evidence.
problem: Since the cloud VM snapshots will be generally of huge size, transferring them elsewhere for processing may lead to the problem of data gravity.

We resolve this problem by designing a framework named SNAPS (Snapshots based Provenance Aware System) which is derived from the existing spatio-temporal models and then customized to suit cloud forensic investigation.

S. No. Challenges in Snapshot acquisition and analysis Sections in which we address these challenges 1 Snapshots act as one of the richest source of evidences during forensic investigation but they may not be available in all the cases.
methodology: The difficulties in the issue of data gravity would get worse when the evidences of huge size have to be acquired from the cloud and processed at the investigator's environment. • Anti-forensics: Techniques used as countermeasures for the forensic analysis.

These techniques can complicate the entire forensic process. • Cloud service Provider (CSP): CSP owns and maintains the cloud resources and rent them to the users with less cost. • Intruder: A person who violates the underlying policies of the environment (here cloud) and performs suspicious activities or attacks. • Tenant: A tenant can have a single user or multiple users.

Once any attack or suspicious activity is accomplished by the intruder then his usual task is to follow appropriate anti-forensic techniques through which the possibility of him being caught may be reduced and sometimes made impossible ( Harris, 2006 ).

objective: In this paper, we present a context-dependent computation-offloading model for MCC, which is based on application segments packed into autonomous agents.

In this paper we present a mobile-cloud computation model based on autonomous agent-based application modules, which are augmented with performance checkpoints for self-performance evaluation and integrity verification units for dynamic detection and reporting of code tampering.
problem: Recent work on this problem has resulted in frameworks with various partitioning and optimization techniques.

The other major obstacle for wider adoption of MCC is the security risks associated with sending sensitive data and code 77 (2018) 380-396 381 to an untrusted platform, including but not limited to the following: • Lack of control on resources and multi-tenancy of different users' applications on the same physical machine make cloud platforms vulnerable to attacks ( Feng et al., 2011 ). • In addition to privacy issues, programs running in the cloud are prone to tampering with code, data, execution flow and communication, as well as masquerading attacks ( Chou, 2013 ). • Mobile code can navigate through multiple platforms before returning to the origin, giving rise to the end-to-end security problem, which involves decreasing control with every further hop in the chain of platforms ( Ali et al., 2015 ).
methodology: Recent work on this problem has resulted in frameworks with various partitioning and optimization techniques.

However, most of these frameworks impose strict requirements on the cloud side, such as a full clone of the application code or special application management software, hindering wide applicability in public clouds.

Achieving ultimate flexibility and performance in mobile-cloud computing is contingent upon the availability of a secure computing framework capable of dynamic decision making with regards to the execution location of different program partitions.
contributions: The main contributions of this paper are as follows: • We present an effective and easy-to-adopt dynamic computation offloading model for high-performance MCC. • We present a dynamic performance estimation model that allows for tracking and relocation of offloaded computation to ensure optimal performance under varying mobilecloud context. • We propose a tamper resistance model for MCC that allows for dynamic integrity-checking and migration of offloaded computation to ensure trustworthiness of results received from a cloud platform. • The proposed computation offloading and protection model relies on lightweight, transparent mechanisms for application code transformation/augmentation, allowing for low response time overhead and high interoperability.
